ety: phir˚ = pher˚ = Prk. फेरे(इ) or फेर(इ) = S. पर्ये(ति), rt. इ with परि
def: v.n. To turn, go round, revolve, whirl; to circulate; to turn back, to return; to walk, walk about, walk to and fro; to wander, rove, ramble, stroll; to travel; to turn over, to roll; to turn away, to turn (from, -se), to forsake (in this sense phir-jānā is the common form); to change; to turn aside, to deviate, wander; to turn, bend, become distorted or crooked, to warp; — to go forth, or to go (to the necessary), to have a stool (usually with jhāṛā or some similar word, e.g. jhāṛā phirnā)
more: v. n. S. स्फर to start. 1. To walk; march; step; pace; tread; tramp; walk about; go round; circulate; whirl; wheel; wander; ramble; stroll; saunter; travel. Phr. To take a walk; take a turn; take a stroll; bend one's steps or course; take the air. Khojī pāṅjī ke chaurā le aulauṅ, dalyā lihalauṅ udhār, Rasoīyā kar-ke pāras lavlauṅ, piyavā phirat bajār . Bhoj. Wom. Song. Some rice I sought and found, some dāl one lent to me, I cooked the mess—my husband's out soiled doves to see! 2. To turn; return; come back. 3. To deviate; diverge; turn or draw aside. Phirūṅ jo tujh se to mujh se merā Khudā phir jāë! Wom. If I turn from you may my God turn from me! 4. To turn from; rise against; revolt; rebel; mutiny ( bāqī honā ). 1. Sārī fauj bādshāh se phir gaī . 2. Ek phirne se tere, sārā zāmānā phir gāyā . 4. To turn aside from one's intention; evade; shuffle; prevaricate. Phir be ghoṛe yahīṅ se ! P…
(perhaps, from Sans. परिक्रम Going round) v. n. To turn, return, walk about, go round, circulate, roll, whirl, to wheel, wander, travel, ramble, change, revolt.
subs: (-se), To turn away from (in dissatisfaction or displeasure), to be dissatisfied, be displeased
(-se), To pass (before); to go back, to return; to turn (from), turn (against), to forsake, abandon, desert, to revolt (from, or against); to be bent, be distorted; to warp.

more: v. n. 1. To slip; slide. Kīchaṛ se har makāṅ kī to bachtā bahut phirā, Par jab dikhāī dī khule bāloṅ kī ek ghaṭā, Bijlī bhī chamkī husn kī, meṅh barsā nāz kā, Phislan jab aisī āī to phir kuchh na bas chalā, Ākhir wahāṅ Nazīr bhī ā-kar phisal paṛā . The rains. Nazīr. I careful picked my steps midst all the slush from every house, But soon my eyes fell on a cloud of tresses black and loose, Quick lightnings flashed, expression's varying hues rained quite a shower, The subtle spell my senses stole, subdued I had no power, In fine, Nazīr too slid into my lady's secret bower. 2. To make a slip; to miss; blunder; err; fail. 3. To incline or be drawn towards; to be taken with. Chiknā dekh phisal paṛī . Prov. On fair outside the senses slide. phisal'nā, phisalnī , adj. Slippery. Kīchaṛ se ho rahī hai jis jā zamīṅ phisalnī, Mushkil huī hai, vāṅ se har ek ko rāh chalnī, Phislā o pāoṅ, pagṛī mushki…

def: v. a. S. स्फुट to burst. 1. To break ( toṛnā ). Kaheṅ Girdhar kabirāë, turat ghar ḍāleṅ phoṛ, Sabhā meṅ baiṭhe tīn jane—chugal, chūtyā, chor . Girdhar Kuṅḍalyā. more: n. m. S. फुल्ल a full-blown flower. 1. A flower; a blossom. Kabhī phūl kī chhaṛī bhī nahīṅ mārī . 2. A boss; knob, stud; rosette. 3. A spark. Chirāg meṅ se phūl jhaṛte haiṅ . 4. The menses. Har mahīne meṅ kuṛhāte the mujhe phūl ke din, Bāre ab-ke to mereṭal gaë māmūl ke din . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Each month the monthly flow to me brought grief, At last this time its stoppage brings relief. 5. The bones of a corpse after partial cremation. 6. A white metal; purified bell-metal. 7. Any light thing; a small quantity. Bhaṅg ke do phūl denā . 8. The white marks of leprosy. 9. (P1.) A Mah. ceremony performed on the third day after death. 10. The best kind of native wine. phūl, phūl sā, halkā phūl , adj. Light as a flower; very light. phūl ānā , v. n. To have the menses ( kapṛoṅ se honā ). phūl jhaṛ'nā , v. n. 1. To drop flowers of rhetoric. Bāt kyā karte haiṅ, muṅh se phūl jhaṛte haiṅ . 2. To fall fro…

s. m. 1. A flower, a blossom; boss, stud, bunch of ribands, &c. a cockade. 2. The menses, of which children are the fruit. 3. A swelling. 4. ety: phūṅk˚ = Prk. फुक्क(इ) or फुक्के(इ), fr. S. फूत्+कृ;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To blow, blow on (with the breath); to puff; to blow (a wind instrument); to blow up (fire, &c.); to set on fire, inflame, kindle; to blow or breathe a charm or incantation
more: v. a. 1. To blow with the breath. Dūdh kā jalā chhāch phūṅk phūṅk pītā hai . Prov. Who've been once scalded by hot milk blow ere they drink even buttermilk. (A burnt child dreads the fire). 2. To blow (a horn, trumpet, etc). Are papīhā, bāore, ādhī rain na kūk, Haure haure sulagtī, taiṅ kyoṅ dīnī phūṅk? Dohā. O cuckoo mad, why in the midnight wilt thou cry? I smould'ring slowly burn, why blazing match apply? 3. To blow a charm or spell; to exorcise. Jāne kyā phūṅk mār dī hai, dīvānah ho gayā . 4. To lavish; waste; throw away. 1. Ham ne saiṅkṛoṅ rupaë phūṅk dīye . 2. Ghar phūṅk tamāshā! Prov. Your house must burn if pleasure you would learn. (You must spend freely if you would know what enjoyment is). 5. To set fire to; blow up. 2. Ranthī sāji chale bandu bhāī, Phūṅk diye koī, koī soṅp diye dharnī . Kabir. Your bier laid out, your brethren bear you on your way, Some fire apply, some in th…

def: v.n. To quiver, tremble, shiver; to flutter; to flap, to wave (as a flag, &c.); see phaṛphaṛānā.
more: v. n. H. phaṭ phaṭ . 1. To flap the wings; flutter. Kān phaṛ phaṛā ḍālo . 2. To move with convulsive motion; to struggle; to be agitated. Pāī khabar jo āmad-i-fasl-i-bahār kī: Kyā phaṛ-phaṛā-ke murg-i-giraftār rah gayā? Rind. Spring's harbinger a joyful message brings, Imprisoned birds are fluttering their wings! 3. To long for; to be anxious; to pine for.
v. n. 1. To tremble. 2. To wave (as, hair in the wind).

def: s.f. Trembling, quivering; tremour, palpitation; flutter

def: s.f. Activity, quickness, alertness, briskness, nimbleness, speed; alacrity, promptness, readiness
more: n. f. S. स्फूर्ति. 1. Quickness; alertness; alacrity; speed; briskness; promptness; promptitude; posthaste. Bandar kī turat, phurat, surat mashhūr hai . Prov. The monkey's cunning and agility are notorious. 2. Readiness; nimbleness; expedition. phurtī se, turat phurat , adv. Quickly; instantly. Phr. In double quick time; on eagle's wing. phurtī karnā , v. n. To make haste; look sharp; to be quick, active, stirring, on the alert; to cut along; dash along; keep moving; push on; go ahead. Phr. To stir one's stumps; to mend one's pace; to put one's best beg foremost.
(from پهرنا) part. f. or s. f. Returning, return, homeward-bound. پهرتي کابهاڙا phirtī kā bhāṛā, The hire of a return boat, carriage, &c.

more: adj. H. phaṭnā to be separated. Single; odd; uneven; unmatched. phuṭ gāṅoṅ , n. m. A detached or isolated village.

intj. S. स्फिट to despise. Fie! shame! for shame! out upon you! 1. Kyā kahūṅ aur, be-hayā tujh ko, Phiṭṭe mūṅh, lānat-i-khudā tujh ko! Wom. Shauq. 2. Phiṭ jīvan us mard kā jis ghar chhoṭī joë, Ek oṛ ko paṛ rahe, khāṭ ṭaṭolan hoë . Play. Chaubolā. A shameful lot that man's is sure whose wife is all too small, Rolled up somewhere she is, he gropes and feels the bed o'er all. phiṭ phiṭ karnā , v. a. To sneer at; taunt; speak out; cry shame on. Phiṭ phiṭ kare use sab dunyā, Jo dhandā kar khāve nā . Rus. Song. The world cries shame upon the man, Who works not but eats all he can.

def: s.f. Crack, split, break, flaw; opening, gap, breach, fissure, rift; separation; cracked or broken vessels (of metal, or crockery); — difference (of opinion), misunderstanding, discord, dissension, split, quarrel, feud
more: s. f. 1. A kind of melon (Cucumis momordica: Roxb. ). 2. A ripe cucumber bursting elastically (as the Cucumis utilatissimus: Roxb. and Momordica mixta: Roxb. ). 3. ( स्फुट ) Odd, unpaired. 4. Difference of opinion, dissension, discord. 5. Separation. 6. A flaw, breach, break. پهوٿ بکهيرا phūṭ bikherā, adj. Scattered, dispersed. پهوٿ پڙنا phūṭ paṛnā, To arise (dissension). پهوٿ پهوٿ ياپهوٿ کررونا phūṭ phūṭ or phūṭ kar ronā, To weep excessively, to burst into tears. پهوٿ رهنا phūṭ rahnā, 1. To be broken. 2. To be dispersed. 3. To be unpaired. پهوت هونا phūṭ honā, To be divided in opinion.
subs: (-meṅ), Dissension to arise or take place (between or among), to disagree, be divided (in opinion)
(-meṅ), To sow discord (between or among), to set by the ears

def: s.f. The inusk melon ( = phūṭ, q.v.). adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
adj. Odd, separated, &c. = phuṭ-kar, phuṭ; — separate, incurred at different places (as debts), sundry, miscellaneous, odds and ends of.

adj. 1. Single. See phuṭ. Ajab tarah kī ye raṅgīṅ chaupaṛ, garaz bichhāī hai yāṅ Khudā ne, Koī hai phuṭkal, kisī kā jug hai, phireṅ haiṅ nardeṅ ye khāne khāne, Jo pāsā pheṅkeṅ banā-banā-kar, aur dāoṅ kitne hī dil meṅ ṭhāṅeṅ, Jo chāhtā hai aṭhārah āveṅ, to uske paṛte haiṅ tīn kāne . (The world compared to a chessboard). Nazīr. A wondrous chequered board diverse this earth by God laid out, Some odd, some even, wandering are from square to square about, Some throw the dice with artful dodge by many thought afore, Who wish eighteen to throw get but three odd ones for their score. 2. Sundry; miscellaneous. Phuṭkal hisāb āj tai kar lo .
adj. See s.v. phut. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
more: n. f. A curse; malediction. phiṭkār barasnā , v. n. To be accursed; to be doomed to wander on the face of the earth.
n. m. 1. The sound of a lash in the air, in winnowing, or in beating clothes against a stone, etc. 2. A long whip used in training horses.

def: adj. Odd, &c. = phuṭ, q.v.; — arrogant, over-bearing. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
more: adj. 1. Odd, unpaired. 2. Separate, dispersed (as, a horseman).

def: adv. = phūṭ, q.v. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
adv. = phūṭ, q.v. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.

more: n. f. The Indian tailor-bird; orthotomus longicauda . Its cry is to-wee-to-wee-to-wee .
n. f. 1. A blot; spot; stain; splash. 2. The undissolved portions of any soluble matter.
n. f. 1. A fowler's net. 2. A large cage. 3. A long rope tied to a rattle to frighten birds.

def: s.f. A blot, spot, stain, speck; splash; — the undissolved portions of any soluble matter.
more: s. f. A blot, spot, stain. ¶ phaṭkī, s. f. 1. A fowlerʼs net. 2. A large cage. 3. A rope tied to a tree to frighten birds with its sound.

adj. Cracked, bad, base (coin).
v.n. = phūṭnā, q.v.
more: v. n. (dakh. i. q. ترقي پانا) To get promotion or advancement (?).
v. n. (dakh.) To rise: see پهوٿنا phūṭnā.

ety: phūṭ˚ = Prk. फुट्ट(इ) = S. स्फुट्य(ते), passive of rt. स्फुट्;+nā = anā = Prk.अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be broken, to be broken into; to be broken down; to be dispersed, be separated, be detached; to separate; to be unpaired; to break, crack, split, burst; to break out or forth; to sprout, shoot, bud, germinate; to burst out or forth, to gush out, to escape (from, -se); to burst out crying; to come into view, to appear, to rise, be spread, be diffused (as a smell, &c.); to be made public, to transpire, to leak out, get wind; to boil (as water); to flourish, get preferment or promotion
more: v. n. 1. To be broken, to be broken into or broken down. 2. To be dispersed, to be separated, to separate, to be unpaired. 3. To burst, split. 4. To be made publick, to transpire. 5. To arise (as, a smell) or to burst forth. 6. To get promotion or advancement.
subs: v.n. (intensive), To break to pieces, to burst asunder, &c.
v.n. To be broken; to be dispersed; to be unpaired.

def: v. n. 1. To crack; split; break; to be rent or broken. 1. Aisī laṛāī huī ki bīsyoṅ sir phūṭ gaë 2. Kitnoṅ ne apne gam se ab hai yeh gat banāī, Maile kuchaile kapṛe, āṅkheṅ bhī ḍabḍabāī, Nā ghar meṅ jhūlā ḍālā, na oṛhnī raṅgāī, Phūṭā paṛā hai chūlhā, ṭūṭī paṛī kaṛāhī. Birāh . The pain of absence. Nazīr. The state of numbers sunk, alas! to that degree, All mean and filthy clad, the tear drops in their ee, Nor swinging in the yard, nor garment of gay dye, The cooking place, the frying-pan, all broken lie. 2. To break out or forth; to have an eruption. Tamām badan phūṭ niklā . 3. To sprout; shoot; germinate. Koṅpal phūṭnā . 4. To spring; burst; gush out; erupt. Pānī phūṭnā . 5. To boil (water). 6. To rise; appear; come into view. 7. To get wind; to be diffused (a smell or report); to be made public. Santan prati baiṭh baiṭh lok-lāj khoī, Ab to bāt phūṭ gaī jānat sab koī, Mere man Rām nām, dūsrā na koī . Mīrāṅ Bāi. (A poetess.) With holy men I've often sat, the world's scorn shown, The secret now is out, by every body known— No other name dwells in my heart save Ram alone. 8. To break silence; utter; speak. Muṅh se itnā bhī na phūṭā 'āiyë' . Raṅgīṅ. 9. To lose an eye; to be blind. Phūṭī saheṅ, āṅjhī na saheṅ . Prov. He would rather lose his eye than spend a farthing on anjan (to save it). 10. To be detached, separated; to deviate, turn aside. phūṭe dīdoṅ, phūṭī nazroṅ , Wom. adv. Not at all. Chhīṅkā, rassī, kanjriyoṅ kā sā yeh laṭkāūṅ maiṅ, nauj! Phūṭī nazroṅ, Chāṅd Khāṅ, bhātā nahīṅ jhūmar mujhe . Wom. Rāhat. phūṭe muṅh se , adv. With a wry face. Ek bosah ham ne māṅgā, rāh-i-maulā, wāh jī! Phūṭe muṅh se yeh na niklā, 'lete jāo, Shāh jī .' I asked her for a kiss—'in God's name grant as charity,' Zounds Sir! not even crossly would she say 'take it, Shah Ji.'
adv. = phūṭ-phūṭ, q.v.s.v. phūṭnā.
adj. Odd, unpaired, &c. = phuṭ, q.v. adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.
s.f. Small fine rain, drizzle; mist, fog; small spot or drop
more: n. f. Small drops of rain; drizzle; Scotch mist. phuār-dār bābarlaiṭ , n. f. Small spotted bobbinet. phuār paṛnā , v. n. To fall in small drops; to drizzle.

n. f. 1. A sloven; slattern; slipshod. 1. Phūaṛ chāle, nau ghar hāle . Prov. When slipshods walk nine houses shake. 2. Aë Chail suhāvan, phūaṛ mail chhuṛāvan . Prov. Now blooming April's come, washed out the slattern's scum (with perspiration). 2. A fool; a stupid; ninny; a silly. 1. Badlī meṅ din na dīse, phūaṛ baiṭhī pīse . Prov. For the clouds, she cannot see it's day, and so the booby grinds and grinds all day. 2. Phūaṛ kā māl haṅs haṅs khāo . Prov. A fool's the owner, ha-ha-ha! we will enjoy it for him, ha-ha-ha! 3. ( phūṛā ). One given to talk obscenely. phūaṛ-pan , n. m. Slovenliness; untidiness; awkwardness.

n. m. P. پيچيدن to twist. 1. Entanglement. See uljhāo (1—3). Pagyā bāṅdhe, pech saṅvāre, tel lagāve julphan meṅ; Tere dayā dharam nahīṅ man meṅ; Mukhṛā kyā dekhe darpan meṅ? Kabīr. Your turban you bind, its folds you adjust, and you oil and you smoothen your hair; Nor virtue nor love in your heart, your face to admire in your glass all your care. 2. A roll of cloth, etc. 3. Windings. See pher . 4. Tactics; stratagem ( chāl ). 5. Equivoque; ambiguity; duplicity. 6. Any trick in wrestling. 7. A screw; ( Pop .) a machine. pech paṛnā , v. n. 1. To become complicated, difficult or intricate. 2. To be entangled. pech denā , v. n. To circumvent; twist; screw; deceive. pech kā chhallā , A thread of a screw. pech khāë huë , ( Bot .) Convolute ( bal-dār ).

(from پيچيدن) s. m. f. 1. Twist, revolution, a coil, a plait, fold. 2. A screw. 3. Perplexity, ambiguity, maze, difficulty, trouble. 4. Deceit. 5. Twisted, coiled, spiral, crooked, intricate. پيچ درپيچ pech-dar-pech, Coil within coil, entangled, complex, intricate. پيچ دينا pech denā, To circumvent, to deceive, to twist, to screw, &c. پيچ ڐالنا pech ḍālnā, 1.
